purpose: High-level overview of Synapse's layered configuration system across three runtime contexts: Node.js SSE server, Electron desktop shell, and Vite-built React frontend. Documents the five configuration layers (server constants, Electron settings, build config, CSS design tokens, project config), how configuration loads at server/Electron/frontend startup, configuration precedence rules for port/settings/project path, key files at-a-glance table, and minimal environment variable usage (PORT, ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE).
gotchas: Server timing constants are hardcoded -- only PORT is overridable via env var
No .env file is used -- server runs with zero configuration
Electron settings store accepts unknown keys for extensibility (not schema-locked)
patterns: Layer separation: server / electron / build / CSS / project config
Defaults-first with override layer (env var, persisted JSON, runtime arg)
